landing.navFeaturesPlatformDesc: Registration, schedule, live standings, protocols — the full Goality stack.
landing.navFeaturesDrawShowDesc: Beautiful tournament draw, as a shareable live show.
landing.navAboutDesc: Tournament Management Cloud — 22 deep-dive pages on what Goality does, end to end.
landing.heroSubtitle: The all-in-one platform for youth football tournaments
landing.heroDesc: From club registration to hotel assignments, payments and schedules — Goality TMC handles everything so organizers can focus on the game, not the spreadsheets.
landing.productDesc: Goality TMC is a cloud-based tournament management system designed specifically for youth football. We eliminate manual work, reduce errors, and create a seamless experience for organizers, clubs, and players.
landing.vp1Desc: Set up your tournament, add age classes, configure services and open registration — all in under 10 minutes. No IT knowledge required.
landing.vp2Desc: Support for multiple languages (EN / RU / ET), currencies, and international club registration. Built for tournaments worldwide.
landing.vp3Desc: Role-based access control — super admin, org admin, club managers. Your data is safe, your workflow is structured.
landing.orgDesc: Stop juggling spreadsheets, WhatsApp groups, and email threads. Goality TMC gives you a single command center to manage everything — before, during, and after the tournament.
landing.f1Desc: Self-service portal for clubs to register, add teams, upload badges and set login credentials.
landing.f2Desc: Bundle accommodation, meals, transfers and extras into packages with per-team pricing.
landing.f3Desc: Assign rooms to teams, track occupancy, manage check-in/check-out and special requests.
landing.f4Desc: Organize bus routes, assign teams to vehicles, track arrival and departure logistics.
landing.f5Desc: Monitor invoices, record payments, calculate balances and export financial summaries.
landing.f6Desc: Send announcements, updates and tournament info directly to all clubs or specific teams.
landing.f7Desc: Clubs add players, staff and accompanying persons with full profile and medical info.
landing.f8Desc: Match schedule, results and tournament protocols — all in one place for everyone.
landing.f9Desc: Real-time overview of registrations, payments, accommodation — everything in one screen.
landing.f10Desc: Full support for English, Russian and Estonian — automatically adapts to the user's locale.
landing.f11Desc: Super admin, org admin, club manager — each with the exact permissions they need.
landing.f12Desc: Manage multiple tournaments under one organization with shared settings and branding.
landing.p1After: ✅ Digital registration — clubs apply online in 3 minutes
landing.p2After: ✅ Visual room dashboard with drag-and-drop assignment
landing.p3After: ✅ Automatic package pricing, invoices and payment tracking
landing.p4After: ✅ Built-in club inbox with read receipts
landing.p5Before: ❌ No visibility on who paid and how much
landing.p5After: ✅ Real-time financial dashboard with balance per team
landing.p6Before: ❌ Players list collected via PDF forms
landing.p6After: ✅ Clubs manage own rosters — players, staff, medical data
landing.trustQuote: Finally a system that understands what tournament organizers actually need. Setup took 20 minutes, clubs loved the self-service registration.
landing.ctaDesc: Join organizers across the world who trust Goality TMC to deliver flawless tournaments. Free to start, scales with your event.
landing.ctaDisclaimer: No credit card required · Setup in 10 minutes · Cancel anytime

features.protocolPlan: Full protocol — Pro and Elite plans
features.lifecycleDesc: Goality covers the full tournament lifecycle. From the first team application to the awards ceremony — in one system.
features.servicesDesc: The organizer can publish all necessary information right on the tournament page — fields, hotels, transfers, services.
features.serviceFieldsDesc: Addresses, map links, notes for each tournament field
features.serviceAccommodationDesc: List of recommended hotels with contacts and prices
features.serviceServicesDesc: Food, kits, equipment — all in one tournament section
features.serviceFinanceDesc: Entry fees, team payments, financial reporting
features.serviceCommunicationDesc: Send messages to all teams or selected ones. Schedule changes, important news — instantly
features.serviceDocumentsDesc: Upload PDF documents directly into the system. Teams download regulations from the tournament page
features.serviceRegistrationDesc: Online registration form, application confirmation, participant management
features.unique1Desc: The only platform that combines registration, format builder, live protocol, finance, and communication in one product. Competitors offer only separate modules.
features.unique2Desc: Tournament from zero to public page — in 10 minutes. No spreadsheets, chats, or manual counting. Everything automatic.
features.unique3Desc: Organizer, club, team, player, and fan — each has their own interface, but all data is shared and updates in real time.
features.unique4Desc: Public pages update every 15 seconds. Fans see the score live — without page reload.
features.unique5Desc: Elite Swiss format, custom phases, Gold/Silver/Bronze tracks — configured in minutes by clicks, without programming.
features.unique6Desc: From 8 teams in a first tournament to 200+ in a federal league. The system works the same at any scale.

features.drawShowFeature1: Groups, league round-robin, playoff brackets — one tool, three formats.
features.drawShowFeature2: Custom branding: tournament logo, division name, team crests + flags.
features.drawShowFeature3: Schedule a premiere — visitors see a countdown, then the show auto-plays.

pricing.heroSubtitle: No monthly commitments. Create a tournament — choose a plan — pay. Host once a year or every week — pay only for results.
pricing.heroNote: All tournaments on Goality · First tournament free
pricing.eliteSubDesc: Unlimited tournaments, teams and divisions across the whole organization. Premium grants Elite-level access on every tournament. Worthwhile from 3-4 tournaments per month.
pricing.extraTeamsNote: Extra teams: 16 teams are included in the base price of Starter, Pro and Elite. For each additional team in the tournament: Starter +€1 · Pro and Elite +€2. One-time payment per tournament.
pricing.calcDesc: No hidden fees. Base price + extra teams above 16 = total per tournament. Use the calculator to estimate your budget.
pricing.marketingDesc: Top of catalog, portal banners, mailing to all club database — a separate story for separate money. Leave your email — be first to know about the launch.
pricing.marketingNoSpam: No spam. Only when we launch.
pricing.marketingFeat3Desc: To all clubs on the platform
pricing.faq1Q: Why pay per tournament, not per month?
pricing.faq1A: Because it is fair. You pay only when you host — whether once a year or once a week. For active organizers, the Elite subscription is better value (€249/mo without limits).
pricing.faq2A: 16 teams are included in the base price of each plan. For each team above 16: Starter +€1, Pro and Elite +€2. One-time payment per tournament.
pricing.faq3A: For leagues and federations hosting many tournaments. €249/mo or €1999/year — all tournaments with no limits on teams, divisions or days. Savings from 3-4 tournaments per month.
pricing.faq4Q: When do I pay for a tournament?
pricing.faq4A: When creating a tournament you choose a plan. Payment via Stripe — card or bank transfer. Tournament activates immediately after payment.
pricing.faq5Q: Can I upgrade from Pro to Elite during a tournament?
pricing.faq5A: Yes, upgrade is available at any time. You pay the difference and immediately get all Elite features.
pricing.faq6Q: What is in the Goality catalog?
pricing.faq6A: The catalog is the main showcase of the platform where teams come looking for tournaments. Starter and above are automatically included in the catalog. Pro — priority placement, Elite — top.
pricing.ctaSubtitle: 12 teams, 1 division, 1 day — all free forever. 2-minute registration.

legal.privacy1Body: Goality Sport Group OÜ (registry code 17232252) is a company registered in Estonia that operates the Goality TMC platform — a tournament management system for sports organisations. Our registered address is Tallinn, Estonia.
legal.privacy2Item1: Account data: name, email address, profile picture (via Google or Facebook login)
legal.privacy2Item2: Club and team data: club name, city, country, contact information
legal.privacy2Item3: Tournament data: registrations, match results, standings
legal.privacy2Item4: Usage data: page visits, browser type, IP address (via Google Analytics)
legal.privacy3Body: We use your data to provide and improve our services: account authentication, tournament management, communication about your registrations, and platform analytics. We do not sell your personal data to third parties.
legal.privacy4Body: We use Google and Facebook for social login (OAuth 2.0). By logging in through these services, you agree to their respective privacy policies. We also use Google Analytics for anonymised usage statistics.
legal.privacy5Body: We retain your personal data for as long as your account is active. If you request deletion, we will remove your personal data within 30 days. Some data may be retained longer if required by law.
legal.privacy6Body: For any questions about your personal data or to exercise your rights under GDPR, please contact us:
legal.deletionIntro: You have the right to request deletion of your personal data that Goality Sport Group OÜ holds about you. This page explains how to submit a deletion request.
legal.deletionWhat1: Your account information (name, email, profile picture)
legal.deletionWhat3: Any preferences and settings associated with your account
legal.deletionStep1: If you signed in via Google or Facebook, revoke the Goality app access in your social account settings.
legal.deletionStep2pre: Send a deletion request email to
legal.deletionStep2post: with the subject line "Data Deletion Request" and your account email address.
legal.deletionStep3: We will confirm receipt and process your request within 30 days.
legal.deletionTimeBody: We will process all deletion requests within 30 days of receipt. You will receive a confirmation email when the process is complete.
legal.termsWarningBody: By using Goality TMC you agree to these Terms in full. If you do not agree, you must stop using the platform immediately.
legal.terms1Body: Goality Sport Group OÜ (17232252) ("Goality", "we", "us") provides Goality TMC — a software-as-a-service platform for managing sports tournaments, including team registration, scheduling, results tracking, and communications. We provide the technology platform only. We are not a tournament organiser, sports body, or event operator.
legal.terms2Body: By registering an account, accessing, or using the platform, you confirm that:
legal.terms2Item1: You are at least 18 years of age or have legal authority to act on behalf of an organisation
legal.terms2Item2: You have read and understood these Terms
legal.terms2Item3: You agree to use the platform only for lawful purposes
legal.terms2Item4: If you use the platform on behalf of an organisation, you have authority to bind that organisation to these Terms
legal.terms3Body: As a user of Goality TMC you are solely responsible for:
legal.terms3Item1: The accuracy and legality of all data you enter into the platform (team names, player data, results, etc.)
legal.terms3Item2: Compliance with all applicable laws in your jurisdiction, including data protection, sports regulations, and consumer protection laws
legal.terms3Item3: How you configure, manage, and conduct tournaments using the platform — Goality has no involvement in the actual conduct of tournaments
legal.terms4Title: Disclaimer of Liability for Tournament Operations
legal.terms4Body: Goality TMC is a software tool. We provide infrastructure — we do not participate in, supervise, organise, endorse, or control any tournament, match, event, or activity conducted through the platform.
legal.terms4DisclaimerBody: Goality Sport Group OÜ accepts no responsibility or liability whatsoever for: disputes between clubs, teams, or players; injuries, accidents, or incidents at events; incorrect results entered by users; disqualifications, scheduling errors, or organisational decisions made by tournament operators; any loss, damage, or harm arising from how the platform is used by any party.

legal.terms5Item2: Use the platform to process data for any purpose other than sports tournament management
legal.terms5Item3: Attempt to gain unauthorised access to other users' data or accounts
legal.terms6Body: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law:
legal.terms6Disclaimer: Goality Sport Group OÜ shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, or any loss of profits, revenue, data, or goodwill, arising out of or in connection with your use of the platform, even if we have been advised of the possibility of such damages. Our total aggregate liability to you shall not exceed the total fees paid by you to Goality in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.
legal.terms7Body: Goality TMC is a digital subscription service. All payments are processed electronically.
legal.noRefundsBody: In accordance with the EU Consumer Rights Directive (2011/83/EU) Article 16(m), the right of withdrawal does not apply to digital content that has been fully performed with your prior express consent. By activating a paid plan on Goality TMC, you expressly consent to immediate performance and acknowledge that you lose your right of withdrawal. All payments are non-refundable. If you believe there is an error in billing, contact us within 14 days of the charge.

Any disputes arising under these Terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Estonia. drawLanding.metaTitle: Draw Show — Beautiful tournament draws in minutes
drawLanding.metaDescription: Create a presentation-ready group draw or playoff bracket for your tournament. No spreadsheets, no random generators — a real show for your teams and audience.
drawLanding.heroTitle: The most beautiful way to present your tournament draw.
drawLanding.heroSubtitle: Turn a flat list of teams into a live, animated draw your players, parents and sponsors will remember. No accounts, no software — just paste, pick a format, and start the show.
drawLanding.includedInPlan: Running your tournament on Goality TMC? Draw Show is <plan>included in Pro and Elite</plan> — no extra charge.
drawLanding.mockupCaption: Live preview — auto-playing demo draw. Your real show will use your team names, logos and branding.
drawLanding.seoBody: Goality Draw Show is a premium online tournament draw maker built for organizers who care about how their event looks. Run a group-stage draw, a full league round-robin schedule, or a knockout playoff bracket — each format turns your team list into a cinematic live show with spotlight reveals, flags, logos and a shareable link. Free to try, built by the same team behind the Goality tournament management platform.
drawLanding.feature1Body: Paste your team list, pick groups or playoff, click Start. That's the whole setup.
drawLanding.feature2Body: Fullscreen stage, spotlight animations, keyboard-driven — perfect for the projector at your opening ceremony.
drawLanding.feature3Body: Every draw gets a URL. Send it to the team WhatsApp, post it, replay it. The show is deterministic — same link, same show.
drawLanding.footerTitle: Goality runs the rest of your tournament too.
drawLanding.footerBody: Registration, accommodation, schedule, live standings, match protocols — all the boring parts, handled. Draw Show is the fun teaser of what the full platform feels like.

auto-schedule.metaTitle: Tournament Schedule Generator — Auto-Build Match Schedules
auto-schedule.metaDesc: Generate full football tournament schedules in seconds. Smart solver handles fields, referees, team blackouts and divisions. Free to try.
auto-schedule.heroSub: What you used to spend three days fixing in Excel — Goality's solver assigns hundreds of matches to fields, slots and referees in a single click.
auto-schedule.heroBullet1: Respects field availability and stadium hours
auto-schedule.heroBullet2: No referee conflicts, no team double-bookings
auto-schedule.heroBullet3: Re-run with one click after any change
auto-schedule.painH2: Hand-built schedules waste days and still break
auto-schedule.painBody: Excel sheets, sticky notes, group-chat ping-pong — and the moment a referee drops out or a team adds a coach, the whole plan unravels. Tournament directors lose entire weekends to logistics that should take minutes. Goality's solver was built so you never re-shuffle a bracket by hand again.
auto-schedule.f1Desc: Reads every constraint — field availability, referee shifts, team blackouts, division order — and produces a feasible plan or tells you exactly why it can't.
auto-schedule.f2Desc: Handles tournaments that span several stadiums and pitches simultaneously, with travel buffers between locations.
auto-schedule.f3Desc: Auto-matches referees to games based on their availability windows and prior assignments — no double-bookings.
auto-schedule.f4Desc: Coaches mark windows when a team can't play (long-distance travel, school events). The solver respects them automatically.
auto-schedule.f5Desc: A team drops out, a field gets rained off, a referee cancels — change the input and regenerate. Existing results are preserved.
auto-schedule.f6Desc: Every match comes with a 'why this slot' explanation, so you can defend the schedule to coaches in seconds.
auto-schedule.q1: How long does the solver take?
auto-schedule.a1: A typical 32-team, 8-field weekend tournament solves in under 30 seconds. Bigger formats — hundreds of matches across several stadiums — usually finish in 1–2 minutes.
auto-schedule.a2: Yes. Pin opening matches, finals, or rivalry games to specific slots and the solver fills the rest around them.
auto-schedule.q3: What happens if there's no feasible solution?
auto-schedule.a3: Instead of returning nothing, the solver surfaces the conflicts: 'Field 2 has only 8 hours, you need 11.' You fix the input and re-run — usually one or two iterations.
auto-schedule.q4: Does it work for round-robin and playoffs?
auto-schedule.a4: Yes — group stages, knockout brackets, and mixed formats are all supported, with auto-advancement from groups to playoffs.
auto-schedule.ctaBody: Spin up your tournament in Goality and let the solver do the heavy lifting. Free tier — no credit card needed.

tournament-website.metaTitle: Tournament Website Builder — Public Page in 5 Minutes
tournament-website.metaDesc: Every tournament gets a polished public website: regulations, schedule, teams, standings, brackets, news. SEO-ready, mobile-first, 4 languages.
tournament-website.heroSub: Regulations, schedule, teams, brackets, standings, news — every Goality tournament gets a polished public website that parents and federations can bookmark.
tournament-website.heroBullet1: Mobile-first, SEO-ready, 4 languages out of the box
tournament-website.heroBullet3: Open Graph cards for clean WhatsApp & social shares
tournament-website.painH2: Your tournament deserves more than a PDF in a WhatsApp group
tournament-website.painBody: Half the chaos of a tournament weekend comes from parents and coaches asking 'where's my next match?'. A printed bracket is out of date the moment a result is logged. Goality turns every tournament into a live website that everyone can refresh — instead of pinging the organizer.
tournament-website.f1Desc: Long-form regulations in a clean reader plus downloadable PDFs — the same source coaches will reference all weekend.
tournament-website.f2Desc: Every match, every field, with status: upcoming, in play, finished. Updates the moment a result is logged.
tournament-website.f3Desc: Group tables and playoff brackets recompute automatically when results come in — no spreadsheet to update.
tournament-website.f4Desc: Public team pages with logo, players (privacy-aware) and head-to-head history. Built for scouts and federations.
tournament-website.f5Desc: Push announcements to followers — schedule changes, results, finals. Parents subscribe with one tap.
tournament-website.f6Desc: English, Russian, Estonian and Spanish out of the box. Custom OG cards mean WhatsApp previews look professional.
tournament-website.q1: Can I customize colours and the cover image?
tournament-website.a1: Yes. Upload a cover, logo and brand colour, and the entire public site picks them up — header, OG card, theme.
tournament-website.q2: Do I need a separate domain?
tournament-website.a2: No. You get a clean URL on goalityfootball.com out of the box. Custom domains are available on Elite plans.
tournament-website.a3: Yes — server-side rendering, structured data, sitemap, multi-language hreflang and clean URLs are built in. We optimise for Google's tournament-search intent.
tournament-website.q4: What about privacy of underage players?
tournament-website.a4: Personal data is hidden by default. Public roster shows only first name + jersey, and parents can opt out per player.
tournament-website.ctaH2: Give your tournament a real home
tournament-website.ctaBody: No website team, no design contractor — just spin up your tournament and the page is live.

team-registration.metaTitle: Online Tournament Registration for Football Teams
team-registration.metaDesc: Take team entries online: clubs self-register, pay the fee, add players. Real-time dashboard, no email back-and-forth, no missed deadlines.
team-registration.heroSub: Clubs register their team, pay the fee, add their players and pick services — all from one link. You watch the dashboard fill in real time.
team-registration.heroBullet1: One public link per tournament — share it anywhere
team-registration.heroBullet3: Per-team caps, deadlines, division gates — enforced automatically
team-registration.painH2: Manual registration is a bottleneck disguised as 'just emails'
team-registration.painBody: Tournament directors lose hours copying team names into a spreadsheet, chasing missing player lists, and reconciling who paid against bank statements. Goality replaces that with a single page where clubs fill in their own data, pay online, and instantly land in your tournament — fully tagged and validated.
team-registration.f1Desc: One link → a club registers a team, adds coaches, picks a division, and pays. You only see clean rows in the dashboard.
team-registration.f2Desc: Hard close on a date, cap by division, cap by total teams — Goality stops registration the moment limits are hit, with a clear public message.
team-registration.f3Desc: After registering, clubs add players, medical info, jersey numbers and required documents — all on the same workspace.
team-registration.f4Desc: Issue codes for early-bird, sponsored teams or partner clubs. Each code can be capped and time-limited.
team-registration.f5Desc: Every registration attempt is logged — including failed payments and abandoned forms — so you know exactly where to follow up.
team-registration.f6Desc: Forms display in the visitor's language. International tournaments stop losing entries to language friction.
team-registration.q1: Can a club register multiple teams?
team-registration.a1: Yes. One coach can manage U10, U12 and U14 teams from the same dashboard and switch between them with a click.
team-registration.q2: What happens if a payment fails?
team-registration.a2: The team stays in 'pending' state, the coach gets clear feedback, and the slot is held until your configurable expiry. You see everything in the registrations log.
team-registration.a3: Yes. Switch a tournament to 'review' mode and you accept or reject each application before the team is published.
team-registration.a4: Refunds run through Stripe and update the registration status automatically. The team sees the refund in their workspace.
team-registration.ctaBody: Pick a deadline, set the fee, share the link. Coaches do the rest.

draw-show.metaTitle: Tournament Draw Show — Live Group Stage Reveal
draw-show.metaDesc: Run your group-stage draw as a polished live show: full-screen presentation, countdown, animated reveals, shareable link. Built for stages and streams.
draw-show.heroSub: Full-screen presentation mode with countdown, animated reveals, partner branding and a shareable link. The moment fans remember from your tournament.
draw-show.heroBullet1: Stream-ready full-screen UI with one-click present mode
draw-show.heroBullet2: Countdown, music cues, animated team reveals
draw-show.heroBullet3: Public link — federations, sponsors and fans watch live
draw-show.painH2: The draw is the moment fans wait for — don't bury it in a spreadsheet
draw-show.painBody: Most tournaments announce groups with a PDF in a chat. Goality's Draw Show turns the moment into a real on-stage event — for free if you already use Goality, with stream-ready visuals, presenter controls and group boards that fill in front of the audience.
draw-show.f1Desc: One-click present mode designed for projectors and live streams. No browser chrome, no distractions.
draw-show.f2Desc: Promote the draw with a public countdown page in your tournament colours. Sponsors, partner logos, embedded video.
draw-show.f3Desc: Teams slide into groups with timed animations. Music cues, hold-to-reveal — fully presenter-controlled.
draw-show.f4Desc: Drive the show from a phone or laptop. Keyboard shortcuts for forward / back / reveal — designed for live MCs.
draw-show.f5Desc: Fans, federations and sponsors watch from any device. Replay link stays live after the event.
draw-show.f6Desc: Issue free passes for sponsors or run the draw as a paid stream. Stripe checkout and discount codes built in.
draw-show.q1: Can I run the draw without a full Goality subscription?
draw-show.a1: Yes. Draw Show is available as a stand-alone product — even tournaments managed elsewhere can use it.
draw-show.a2: Yes. Upload partner logos, change colours, add a sponsor stinger before the reveal.
draw-show.a3: Groups are written directly back into your tournament — no manual transfer. The shareable link keeps working as a replay.
draw-show.q4: Can the draw be random or rigged with rules?
draw-show.a4: Both. Pure random, seeded random, or rule-based (no two teams from the same country in the same group) — all supported.
draw-show.ctaH2: Give your draw the moment it deserves
draw-show.ctaBody: Set up your Draw Show in minutes — and turn a five-minute admin task into the highlight of your tournament week.

live-standings.metaDesc: Goals, cards and substitutions sync live to standings and brackets. Public refresh, push notifications and instant playoff advancement.
live-standings.heroH1Highlight: the second a goal is scored
live-standings.heroSub: Log a goal in the referee panel and the public site, group tables, brackets and follower notifications all update in real time.
live-standings.heroBullet1: Goals, cards and subs streamed live to the public site
live-standings.heroBullet3: Tie-breakers, head-to-head and goal difference handled automatically
live-standings.painH2: Updating tables by hand is how mistakes go public
live-standings.painBody: One wrong cell in a spreadsheet, one missed tie-breaker — and a team is told they're out when they're actually in. Goality recomputes standings from the source of truth: the match events you log, with full rule support for points, head-to-head, goal difference and disciplinary tie-breakers.
live-standings.f1Desc: Goals, cards, substitutions and incidents go straight into the platform — from the referee panel, the protocol screen or the admin app.
live-standings.f2Desc: Group standings update on every event. Configurable tie-breakers: points, head-to-head, goal difference, disciplinary, draw.
live-standings.f3Desc: When a group stage ends, top finishers slot into the bracket according to your qualification rules — no manual entry.
live-standings.f4Desc: Fans following a team get a notification when their team scores or finishes — without you sending anything.
live-standings.f5Desc: Every score change is logged with timestamp, user and old/new values. Disputes are resolved in seconds.
live-standings.f6Desc: Knockout brackets render publicly and update with every result — quarter-finals fill from group winners automatically.
live-standings.a1: Public pages refresh on every event log — typically within a few seconds of the referee tapping 'goal'.
live-standings.a2: Points → head-to-head → goal difference → goals scored → fair-play (disciplinary points) → draw. The order is configurable per tournament.
live-standings.q3: Can I edit a score after the match?
live-standings.a3: Yes. Edits are logged in the result audit log with who and when, and standings recompute automatically.
live-standings.a4: The referee panel is designed for spotty stadium connectivity: events queue locally and sync the moment the network returns.
live-standings.ctaBody: Stop emailing PDF tables. Goality keeps the truth in one place — and shows it to everyone, the second it happens.

organizer-dashboard.metaTitle: Tournament Organizer Dashboard — Manage Every Cup in One Place
organizer-dashboard.metaDesc: One dashboard for every tournament you run — registrations, payments, schedules, teams. Setup checklist keeps nothing slipping. Free to start.
organizer-dashboard.heroSub: Switch between cups, see registrations stack up, track revenue and finish the setup checklist — without 12 tabs and 4 spreadsheets.
organizer-dashboard.heroBullet1: Multi-tournament dashboard with revenue, teams and check-in stats
organizer-dashboard.heroBullet2: Setup checklist guides each tournament from zero to public launch
organizer-dashboard.heroBullet3: One inbox for coach messages across all your cups
organizer-dashboard.painH2: Running 3+ cups a season? Excel can't keep up
organizer-dashboard.painBody: Tournament directors juggle parallel registrations, parallel schedules, parallel reconciliations. The moment one tournament gets organized, the next one is already a mess. Goality's organizer dashboard is built for parallel workload — every tournament is a card, every step is checked, every signal surfaces before it becomes a fire.
organizer-dashboard.featuresH2: Built for organizers running serious volume
organizer-dashboard.f1Desc: All your tournaments on one screen — status, teams confirmed, revenue, days to kick-off. Spot what's stalling in 5 seconds.
organizer-dashboard.f2Desc: Each tournament has a built-in checklist: regulations, divisions, stadiums, registration link, payments, schedule. Nothing ships half-baked.
organizer-dashboard.f3Desc: See every new team registration in real time, grouped by tournament and division. Drill in to see who paid and who didn't.
organizer-dashboard.f4Desc: Aggregate revenue across tournaments, paid vs outstanding, refunds, top services sold. Numbers organizers actually need.
organizer-dashboard.f5Desc: Messages from coaches across all your tournaments land in one inbox, tagged by tournament. Reply once, fly on.
organizer-dashboard.f6Desc: Switch between Stripe test and live with one click. Onboard a new organizer, take fake payments, ship live the moment you're ready.
organizer-dashboard.q1: Can multiple admins share one dashboard?
organizer-dashboard.a1: Yes. Invite co-organizers with role-based access — read or write, per tournament. Everyone sees the same source of truth.
organizer-dashboard.a2: Yes. The dashboard is mobile-first — most operations work from a phone, and a dedicated tournament hub view surfaces the day's critical actions.
organizer-dashboard.q3: Can I clone a tournament from last season?
organizer-dashboard.a3: Yes. Duplicate any tournament — regulations, divisions, services, pricing — and tweak only what changed.
organizer-dashboard.a4: Free tier includes a fully working dashboard. Higher tiers unlock more tournaments, more divisions and the Elite organization-wide subscription.
organizer-dashboard.ctaBody: Bring all your tournaments into one cockpit. Free tier, no card.

divisions.metaTitle: Divisions & Age Groups — U10, U12, Women's Brackets in One Tournament
divisions.metaDesc: Run U10, U12, U14, Women and Pro brackets inside a single tournament. Parallel standings, separate fees, shared logistics — fully integrated.
divisions.heroSub: U10, U12, U14, Women, Pro — every age group is a parallel bracket inside the same cup, with separate standings, fees and rosters.
divisions.heroBullet1: Independent groups and playoffs per division
divisions.heroBullet2: Per-division pricing, caps and registration deadlines
divisions.painH2: Trying to run U10 and U14 in 'one tournament' on a spreadsheet?
divisions.painBody: Mixing age groups in a spreadsheet means one wrong filter and U10 teams land in the U14 schedule. Goality models divisions as first-class objects — each with its own fee, cap, format, group stage and playoff — so the only thing your cup shares is the date and the hotel.
divisions.f1Desc: Each division has its own format: round-robin for U8, groups + playoffs for U14, single-elimination for adults. All inside one tournament.
divisions.f2Desc: U10 fee, U14 fee, women's fee — set them independently. Apply discounts to one division without touching others.
divisions.f3Desc: U12 fills up faster than U16? Close registrations on the popular division while keeping others open.
divisions.f4Desc: Mark divisions as male / female / mixed. Validate player ages against division rules at registration time.
divisions.f5Desc: Each division has its own live table and bracket on the public website — fans see exactly what they came for.
divisions.f6Desc: Shared accommodation, transfers, opening ceremony — modelled at tournament level. Division logistics stay per-division.
divisions.q1: How many divisions can one tournament have?
divisions.a1: As many as your plan allows. Free starts at one, paid tiers add more — Elite is unlimited.
divisions.a2: Yes. Move a team mid-registration if they were misplaced. The team keeps its players and payment, just lands in the right bracket.
divisions.q3: Do referees need to be split per division?
divisions.a3: Up to you. Pool all referees across the tournament, or restrict a referee to specific divisions (e.g. youth-certified only).
divisions.q4: Can I have different sponsors per division?
divisions.a4: Yes. Each division can carry its own partner logos and OG card — useful when an under-18 league has different sponsors than the senior cup.
divisions.ctaBody: Stop running 4 parallel spreadsheets. Set up your divisions inside Goality and let each bracket live its own life.

stadiums-fields.metaTitle: Stadiums & Fields Management — Multi-Venue Football Tournaments
stadiums-fields.metaDesc: Manage multiple stadiums and pitches inside one tournament. Set availability windows, assign matches, the solver respects every constraint.
stadiums-fields.heroSub: Add every stadium and pitch, set when each one is available, and let the auto-scheduler distribute hundreds of matches across them.
stadiums-fields.heroBullet1: Unlimited stadiums and fields per tournament
stadiums-fields.heroBullet2: Per-field availability windows and surface type
stadiums-fields.heroBullet3: Solver respects travel buffers between venues
stadiums-fields.painH2: Big tournaments span 5 stadiums. Excel doesn't
stadiums-fields.painBody: Once you go past two pitches, the schedule becomes a 3D puzzle: which match on which field at which time without breaking referee shifts. Goality models every field as a first-class entity with its own opening hours, surface type and break slots — and the solver does the puzzle for you.
stadiums-fields.f1Desc: Add a stadium once. Add as many pitches as it has. Each field gets its own size (5v5 / 7v7 / 11v11), surface and label.
stadiums-fields.f2Desc: Set when each field is bookable. Lunch breaks, maintenance slots, evening curfews — all respected by the scheduler.
stadiums-fields.f3Desc: Mark distance between stadiums. The solver adds buffer time when a team plays at venue A then B in the same day.
stadiums-fields.f4Desc: See exactly how full each field is per day. Spot under-used pitches and over-loaded ones before kick-off.
stadiums-fields.f5Desc: Stadiums show up on the public tournament site with map, photos and getting-there notes — parents stop asking.
stadiums-fields.f6Desc: Rain on Field 2? Drag the match to Field 4, the schedule and bracket update everywhere automatically.
stadiums-fields.q1: Is there a limit on stadiums per tournament?
stadiums-fields.a1: Free tier covers one stadium. Higher tiers add more venues — Elite is unlimited. A tournament can span as many stadiums as your plan allows.
stadiums-fields.q2: Can a field be temporarily closed?
stadiums-fields.a2: Yes. Mark a field unavailable for a specific window (rain, maintenance) — existing matches get re-suggested, future scheduling avoids the slot.
stadiums-fields.q3: Does the solver pick the closest field for back-to-back games?
stadiums-fields.a3: Yes — if travel buffers are set, the solver prefers same-stadium scheduling for consecutive matches of the same team.
stadiums-fields.q4: Can I show photos and a map of each stadium?
stadiums-fields.a4: Yes. Each stadium has a public profile on the tournament site — photos, address, map link, parking notes. Built for parents and visiting teams.
stadiums-fields.ctaBody: Set up every venue once, the scheduler reuses them forever. Free tier — no card.

regulations-documents.metaTitle: Tournament Regulations & Documents — One Source of Truth
regulations-documents.metaDesc: Publish tournament rules online, upload official PDFs, attach contracts and waivers. Coaches reference the same source all weekend.
regulations-documents.heroSub: Publish the regulation as a clean web page plus downloadable PDFs. Every coach references the same source — no out-of-date Word files.
regulations-documents.heroBullet2: Multi-PDF upload for contracts, waivers, codes of conduct
regulations-documents.heroBullet3: Public on the tournament site, downloadable in one click
regulations-documents.painH2: Regulation v3-final-FINAL.docx in a WhatsApp group
regulations-documents.painBody: Half the disputes at the registration desk come from a coach citing a regulation that was updated three days ago and the email never reached them. Goality stores the canonical regulation online — readable on a phone, downloadable as PDF — and every coach lands on the latest version, every time.
regulations-documents.f1Desc: A clean reader for the full regulation: headings, lists, tables. Renders beautifully on mobile, no infinite scroll fatigue.
regulations-documents.f2Desc: Attach official PDFs — federation regulation, insurance, code of conduct — directly to the tournament. Single click download for coaches.
regulations-documents.f3Desc: Publish the same regulation in EN / RU / ET / ES. Visitors see their language automatically; international cups stop losing entries.
regulations-documents.f4Desc: Every regulation shows when it was last updated. Coaches see the freshness; you defend the source if questioned.
regulations-documents.f5Desc: Each heading gets its own anchor. Cite 'see section 4.2' in a WhatsApp message and the link drops the reader right there.
regulations-documents.f6Desc: Force coaches to accept the regulation when registering. Acceptance is logged — useful if a dispute reaches a federation review.
regulations-documents.q1: Can I edit the regulation after coaches have agreed to it?
regulations-documents.a1: Yes — but every coach gets a notification when a substantive change lands, and the new version is logged. The original acceptance and the new one are both kept.
regulations-documents.q2: What file types can I upload?
regulations-documents.a2: PDFs (recommended for official documents), images, and standard office formats. PDFs render with a preview on the public site.
regulations-documents.q3: Can the regulation be private until launch?
regulations-documents.a3: Yes. Keep it draft until you're ready to open registrations — then publish in one click.
regulations-documents.q4: Do I need to write the regulation in Goality?
regulations-documents.a4: You can paste from Word/Google Docs and we'll preserve headings, lists and basic formatting. Or just upload a PDF and skip the editor.
regulations-documents.ctaBody: One regulation, one source, every coach on the same page. Free to use.

org-listing.metaTitle: Organization Listing — Your Own Branded Tournament Hub
org-listing.metaDesc: Every organizer gets a public branded URL listing all their tournaments. Custom cover, logo and colors — your own mini league site.
org-listing.heroSub: Every Goality organizer gets a public branded URL (goalityfootball.com/t/yourorg) listing every tournament you've ever run — past, current and upcoming.
org-listing.heroBullet1: Custom cover, logo and brand color
org-listing.heroBullet2: Country and city auto-detected from your tournaments
org-listing.heroBullet3: SEO-ready, indexed by Google for 'your league name'
org-listing.painH2: Your league deserves a real home, not a folder in Google Drive
org-listing.painBody: When a parent searches for your league name on Google, what do they find? A scattered PDF, a Facebook group, an outdated WordPress page? Goality gives every organizer a clean public hub at goalityfootball.com/t/yourorg — branded, indexed and updated automatically as you publish new tournaments.
org-listing.f1Desc: Every tournament you run becomes a card on your org listing — past, current and upcoming, with photos, dates and one-click access.
org-listing.f2Desc: Upload your logo, cover image and accent color. The full org page picks them up — header, OG card, every tournament page.
org-listing.f3Desc: Server-rendered, structured-data tagged, in the sitemap. Search for your league name and Goality is the top result.
org-listing.f4Desc: Each tournament shows its flag, country and host city — international visitors instantly understand your footprint.
org-listing.f5Desc: Your org page renders in EN / RU / ET / ES — same content, different language, automatically. International teams discover you faster.
org-listing.f6Desc: Past tournaments stay public as an archive — full results, photos, winners. Builds credibility for new teams considering you.
org-listing.a1: The slug is yours — pick `kingscup`, `kohtla-cup`, `myleague`. Custom domains (yourleague.com) are available on Elite.
org-listing.a2: Upcoming first, then live, then past — chronological inside each section. You can pin a flagship tournament to the top.
org-listing.q3: Can I hide a tournament from the listing?
org-listing.a3: Yes. Keep a tournament unlisted (still public via direct link) if it's a private invitational.
org-listing.q4: Do I get SEO benefit from past tournaments?
org-listing.a4: Yes — past tournaments build authority for your org page. Search engines see a real org with a track record, not a one-off cup.
org-listing.ctaH2: Give your league a real home
org-listing.ctaBody: One branded URL, every tournament you've ever run. Free to publish.

pricing-tiers.metaTitle: Tournament Software Pricing — Pay Per Tournament, Not Per Year
pricing-tiers.metaDesc: Goality is priced per tournament: Free, Starter €19, Pro €49, Elite €89. Or Elite org subscription €249/mo for unlimited tournaments.
pricing-tiers.heroSub: Free for small cups. €19 for Starter. €49 for Pro. €89 for Elite. Or €249/month for an organization-wide Elite subscription — unlimited tournaments.
pricing-tiers.heroBullet1: Tier per tournament — pay only for what you actually run
pricing-tiers.heroBullet2: Free tier is genuinely free — no credit card required
pricing-tiers.heroBullet3: Elite org subscription covers every tournament you organize
pricing-tiers.painH2: Per-seat SaaS pricing doesn't fit how tournaments work
pricing-tiers.painBody: Most platforms charge per user per month — a model built for offices, not for seasonal tournament organizers. Goality charges per tournament: small cup is free, professional cup is one fixed fee, big cup pays a bit more. If you run a lot of tournaments, the Elite subscription unlocks unlimited usage at one flat monthly price.
pricing-tiers.f1Desc: Real tier — not a 14-day trial. Limited teams, divisions and stadiums, but everything works: registration, payments, schedule, public site.
pricing-tiers.f2Desc: More teams, more divisions, document uploads, branded OG cards. The sweet spot for one-off weekend cups.
pricing-tiers.f3Desc: Multi-stadium, advanced solver, referee management, follow notifications. Pro tournaments that take volunteers' weekends.
pricing-tiers.f4Desc: Unlimited everything per tournament. Big national or international cups with multiple divisions and venues.
pricing-tiers.f5Desc: €249/month or €1999/year covers every tournament you run on Goality, all at Elite level. The choice for federations and recurring leagues.
pricing-tiers.f6Desc: Need one extra team beyond the cap? €1. Extra division? €9. Pay only for the overflow without bumping a whole tier.
pricing-tiers.q1: Do I pay upfront or after?
pricing-tiers.a1: Upfront — the tier unlocks features the moment you pay. Free tier never asks for payment.
pricing-tiers.a2: Yes. Hit a limit on Starter, upgrade to Pro right there and the limit lifts immediately. No refunds for downgrades within an active tournament.
pricing-tiers.q3: What if I run 10 cups a year?
pricing-tiers.a3: Take the Elite organization subscription at €249/month or €1999/year. Every tournament you run is automatically at Elite level — no per-tournament purchase.
pricing-tiers.a4: Yes — full refund within 14 days of purchase if you haven't published the tournament yet. After publication, refunds are case-by-case via Stripe.
pricing-tiers.ctaH2: Pay for what you actually run
pricing-tiers.ctaBody: Start free. Upgrade only when you hit a limit. No long-term lock-in.

match-operations.metaTitle: Match Operations — Live Scoring, Cards, Subs & Protocols
match-operations.metaDesc: Log goals, cards and substitutions live from the referee panel. Print match protocols, audit every change, dispute resolved in seconds.
match-operations.heroSub: Live scoring, cards, subs, incidents — and the printable protocol that every federation expects. From referee panel to public standings, one source of truth.
match-operations.heroBullet1: Goals, cards and subs streamed live to standings and brackets
match-operations.heroBullet2: Printable protocol with signatures and incident log
match-operations.painH2: Paper protocols + WhatsApp scores = disputes you can't defend
match-operations.painBody: A coach claims the second yellow was actually for #7, not #17. The protocol is in someone's backpack, the score in someone's phone, the truth lost to memory. Goality logs every match event with timestamp and user — so when a dispute hits, you have receipts.
match-operations.f1Desc: Goals, yellow/red cards, substitutions, penalties, incidents — each with minute, player, team. Tap-by-tap on the referee panel.
match-operations.f2Desc: Coaches submit their lineup before kick-off. The referee panel shows who's on the pitch, who's on the bench, who's eligible.
match-operations.f3Desc: The official match protocol generates itself from the event log. Print, sign, file — or just keep the digital version with audit trail.
match-operations.f4Desc: Every score change is logged with who and when. Edits stay traceable; the public standings always reflect the latest truth.
match-operations.f5Desc: Stadium connectivity is unreliable. Events queue locally on the referee panel and sync the moment the network returns.
match-operations.f6Desc: Match states (scheduled, in play, paused, finished, disputed) drive the public page automatically — no manual flipping.
match-operations.q1: Can the referee panel work without internet?
match-operations.a1: Yes — designed for it. Events queue locally and sync when the connection comes back, with no data loss.
match-operations.q2: Can I print an official protocol?
match-operations.a2: Yes. The auto-generated protocol matches federation conventions: lineup, events, signatures, incidents — printable as PDF.
match-operations.q3: Who can edit a finished match?
match-operations.a3: Only the tournament director or a delegated admin. Every edit lands in the audit log with user, timestamp and old/new values.
match-operations.q4: How fast do public pages update?
match-operations.a4: Within seconds of the event being logged. Standings, brackets and follower notifications all fire from the same event stream.
match-operations.ctaH2: Run match day like a federation
match-operations.ctaBody: Live scoring, audit-grade protocols, zero spreadsheets. Free to try.

brackets-stages.metaTitle: Tournament Brackets & Stages — Groups + Playoffs Done Right
brackets-stages.metaDesc: Group stages, knockout brackets, auto-advancement, configurable qualification rules — the full tournament format engine.
brackets-stages.heroSub: Run any format: round-robin groups, single-elimination knockout, double-elimination, mixed. Winners advance automatically by your qualification rules.
brackets-stages.heroBullet2: Knockout brackets that fill themselves from group winners
brackets-stages.heroBullet3: Qualification rules — top-N, best 2nd, ranked seeds
brackets-stages.painH2: Drawing brackets in PowerPoint is how you lose a referee's trust
brackets-stages.painBody: After 6 group matches you need 4 quarter-final pairings — and a slide-deck bracket isn't the answer. Goality treats stages as data: group → top-N advances → seeded into the bracket → updates with every result. No manual transcription, no Sunday-morning panic.
brackets-stages.f1Desc: Round-robin groups with auto-computed tables. Configurable tie-breakers (head-to-head, goal difference, fair-play).
brackets-stages.f2Desc: Single-elimination 4 / 8 / 16 / 32 / 64 brackets render publicly and update with every result. Re-seedable if a team withdraws.
brackets-stages.f3Desc: When the group stage ends, winners slot into the bracket by your qualification rules. No spreadsheet, no copy-paste.
brackets-stages.f4Desc: Top-1, top-2, best 2nd-placed teams, ranked seeds across groups — express any qualification logic the federation uses.
brackets-stages.f5Desc: Groups + playoffs, double-elimination, Swiss for early rounds then knockout. Mix and match per division.
brackets-stages.f6Desc: Export the bracket as PDF for printing or sharing with sponsors. Public version always stays in sync online.
brackets-stages.q1: Can I have groups + playoffs with auto-advance?
brackets-stages.a1: Yes — the most common format. Set group size, set qualifying spots, define the bracket and Goality wires them together.
brackets-stages.a2: Supported — winners and losers brackets render side by side, with auto-routing of losers into the losers' bracket.
brackets-stages.a3: Yes. If a team withdraws or a group needs reshuffling, reseed the bracket — affected matches get re-suggested, results stay logged.
brackets-stages.a4: Per tournament: points → head-to-head → goal difference → goals scored → fair-play → draw. Reorder freely.
brackets-stages.ctaBody: Pick a format, the bracket fills itself. Free to use, no card.

referees.metaTitle: Referee Management — Assignments, Availability, Magic Link Login
referees.metaDesc: Manage referees: track availability, assign matches, send a magic-link to their personal panel — no password needed.
referees.heroSub: Add referees, mark their availability windows, assign them to matches — and send each one a magic link to their personal panel. No password, no support tickets.
referees.heroBullet1: Per-referee availability windows the solver respects
referees.heroBullet2: Magic-link login — no password to remember or reset
referees.heroBullet3: Match assignments visible in their dedicated panel
referees.painH2: Referees don't want a login. They want their next match.
referees.painBody: Most platforms expect referees to create an account, set a password, install an app, then find their schedule somewhere. Real referees show up at the stadium, want to know which match is next, and want to log goals on their phone. Goality's referee flow is built for that — one magic link, the day's matches, tap-to-score.
referees.featuresH2: What you get with referee management
referees.f1Desc: Add referees with name, contact, certification level. Optional: photo, languages, age-group restrictions.
referees.f2Desc: Each referee marks when they can work. Solver respects windows; you see conflicts before assignment, not during.
referees.f3Desc: Assign main referee + assistants per match. Bulk-assign by group, by stadium or by day with one click.
referees.f4Desc: Send each referee a personal link. They open it on the phone, see their matches, log events. No password, no app install.
referees.f5Desc: The referee panel is mobile-first: today's matches, tap a match to start scoring. Designed for use in a hand on a windy pitch.
referees.f6Desc: Referees are constraints in the scheduler. Add a referee mid-tournament and re-run — the solver re-balances assignments.
referees.q1: Do referees need a Goality account?
referees.a1: No. They get a personal magic link, open it, and they're in. The link is tied to one tournament and rotates if compromised.
referees.q2: Can a referee work for multiple tournaments?
referees.a2: Yes — each tournament issues its own link. The referee opens whichever link matches the day's tournament.
referees.q3: What if a referee cancels last-minute?
referees.a3: Mark them unavailable for the affected window, re-run the assignment view. Suggested replacements surface immediately based on availability.
referees.q4: Do referees see standings or full results?
referees.a4: By default, no — their panel is scoped to their matches. Optional: open referee role with full read access for senior officials.
referees.ctaBody: One link per referee, all their matches, real-time scoring. Free to start.

services-packages.metaTitle: Services & Packages — Hotel, Meals, Transfers Bundled per Team
services-packages.metaDesc: Build packages (hotel + meals + transfer), set per-division prices, override per team, publish to clubs as a self-serve shop.
services-packages.heroSub: Build packages once, override prices for sponsor teams, publish to coaches as a self-serve shop. Every deal tracked from offer to last payment.
services-packages.heroBullet1: Catalog of services (hotel, meals, transfers, training)
services-packages.heroBullet3: Per-team overrides without breaking the master price list
services-packages.painH2: Pricing 30 teams individually in WhatsApp is how you go broke
services-packages.painBody: Each coach asks: 'is breakfast included?' 'can we swap hotel?' 'do we still get the early-bird?'. With a spreadsheet, the answer changes by who you ask. Goality models every package as data — clean prices, transparent add-ons, overrides logged, payments matched.
services-packages.f1Desc: Add hotels, meals, transfers, training sessions. Each service has name, description, base price, per-player option.
services-packages.f2Desc: Combine services into a package: hotel + 2 meals + airport transfer. Price the package independently from line items.
services-packages.f3Desc: Sponsor team gets free hotel? Override one line for one team without touching the master price. Logged with reason.
services-packages.f4Desc: Apply discounts (early-bird, partner club) or surcharges (single room, dietary) as line-level adjustments. Per-player or flat.
services-packages.f5Desc: Mark a package as published — it appears in the team's self-serve shop. Hide drafts until you're ready.
services-packages.f6Desc: Every team's chosen package, add-ons, adjustments and payments live as one 'deal'. Subtotal, paid, outstanding — at a glance.
services-packages.q1: Can a team add extras after their package is set?
services-packages.a1: Yes. They add extras in their shop view, you see them in the deal. Outstanding balance updates automatically.
services-packages.q2: How do I handle per-player pricing?
services-packages.a2: Each service can have a per-player option (e.g. €15 per player for breakfast). Roster size drives the total automatically.
services-packages.q3: What if a team disputes a charge?
services-packages.a3: Every line, every adjustment and every payment is timestamped and tied to a user. Open the deal — the audit trail tells the full story.
services-packages.q4: Can I copy packages from a previous tournament?
services-packages.a4: Yes. Clone the tournament's offerings as the starting point and tweak — useful when you run the same cup every year.
services-packages.ctaBody: Build packages once. Publish. Let coaches pick and pay.

accommodation-logistics.metaTitle: Accommodation & Logistics — Hotels, Meals, Transfers for Touring Teams
accommodation-logistics.metaDesc: Multi-hotel catalog, per-team bookings, dietary preferences, transfer slots — everything visiting teams need to plan a tournament trip.
accommodation-logistics.heroSub: Catalog the hotels you offer, let teams book directly, capture dietary preferences, manage airport pickups — all inside the same tournament.
accommodation-logistics.heroBullet1: Multi-hotel catalog with photos, rates and locations
accommodation-logistics.heroBullet2: Per-team booking with rooming list and dietary notes
accommodation-logistics.heroBullet3: Transfer slots with pickup time and capacity
accommodation-logistics.painH2: Touring teams ask the same 20 questions. Answer them once.
accommodation-logistics.painBody: International coaches need to know: which hotels, what rate, breakfast included, how far from the pitch, airport pickup window, dietary support. Goality lets you put it all on the tournament site — and accept bookings without a 50-email email chain per team.
accommodation-logistics.f1Desc: Add the hotels you've partnered with. Each gets photos, rate, breakfast info, distance to stadium, contact info.
accommodation-logistics.f2Desc: Teams fill in their rooming list inside their portal — twin rooms, single requests, accompanying parents. Hotel sees a clean roster.
accommodation-logistics.f3Desc: Configure meal options (breakfast, hot lunch, post-match snack). Teams flag vegetarian / halal / allergies per player.
accommodation-logistics.f4Desc: Define transfer slots with capacity. Teams pick arrival and departure slots; the dispatcher sees a clean manifest.
accommodation-logistics.f5Desc: Each team has a travel card: arrival flight, departure flight, special needs. One screen — no PDF attachments.
accommodation-logistics.f6Desc: Hotels, transfers, getting-there guide all show on the tournament public site. Visiting coaches stop asking.
accommodation-logistics.q1: Can teams book rooms without paying me directly?
accommodation-logistics.a1: Yes. You can either collect payment via Goality (Stripe) or just hand off the booking to the hotel — both flows are supported.
accommodation-logistics.q2: How do I handle different hotel tiers?
accommodation-logistics.a2: Add each tier as a service, price it independently, let teams pick. Sponsor teams can be auto-assigned the premium tier with an override.
accommodation-logistics.q3: Can dietary preferences reach the caterer?
accommodation-logistics.a3: Yes. Export per-team or per-day breakdowns of dietary preferences as PDF or CSV for the catering partner.
accommodation-logistics.q4: What about local transfers between stadiums?
accommodation-logistics.a4: Define stadium-to-stadium transfer slots if your tournament spans multiple venues. Teams book in their portal, drivers see manifests.
accommodation-logistics.ctaH2: Plan a touring tournament without the email chain
accommodation-logistics.ctaBody: Hotels, meals, transfers, dietary — one tournament, one source.

team-workspace.metaTitle: Team Workspace — One Portal for Players, Coaches and Logistics
team-workspace.metaDesc: Every team gets its own portal: roster, staff, jerseys, documents, travel and inbox — one screen for everything a club needs.
team-workspace.heroSub: Players, coaches, jerseys, documents, travel, services and a direct line to the organizer — all on one screen, on phone or laptop.
team-workspace.heroBullet1: Players, staff and accompanying parents in one roster
team-workspace.heroBullet2: Jersey numbers, documents and travel in dedicated tabs
team-workspace.heroBullet3: Direct inbox to the tournament organizer
team-workspace.painH2: Coaches shouldn't need a 30-tab browser to manage a team
team-workspace.painBody: Every tournament has its own form, its own deadline, its own email chain. Coaches lose hours juggling info between Excel, WhatsApp and PDFs. Goality's team workspace puts everything a club needs in one portal — players, jerseys, documents, travel, payments — scoped to the tournament you're playing.
team-workspace.f1Desc: Players with photo, position, jersey number, birth date, medical notes. Add by upload or one-by-one, edit on the go.
team-workspace.f2Desc: Head coach, assistants, physio, team manager — each with role and contact. Visible to the organizer if needed.
team-workspace.f3Desc: Parents and travelers attached to the team. Counts toward accommodation, transfer slots and meal totals.
team-workspace.f4Desc: Kit colors, jersey numbers per player, badge upload. Avoids the day-of-tournament 'two teams in white' surprise.
team-workspace.f5Desc: Upload signed waivers and ID copies. Mark arrival / departure flights. Available to the organizer in one click.
team-workspace.f6Desc: Direct messages to the tournament organizer plus the services shop — pick a package, add extras, pay online.
team-workspace.q1: Can a coach manage multiple teams?
team-workspace.a1: Yes. One login, switch between U10, U12, U14 with one click. The workspace re-scopes to the team you've selected.
team-workspace.a2: Optional. The coach can invite parents with read-only access — useful for travel info and dietary forms.
team-workspace.q3: What devices is it built for?
team-workspace.a3: Mobile-first. Most coaches use the workspace from a phone. Desktop is supported with denser layouts for offices.
team-workspace.a4: Yes. Export the roster as PDF or CSV for federations, insurance or visa applications.
team-workspace.ctaH2: Give your team a proper home
team-workspace.ctaBody: Stop juggling Excel and PDFs. One portal, every tournament, your whole club.

players-roster.metaTitle: Player Roster Management — Medical Data, IDs, Federation Exports
players-roster.metaDesc: Manage players with photo, birth date, jersey, medical notes and documents. GDPR-aware, federation-ready, one-click roster exports.
players-roster.heroSub: Photo, birth date, jersey, medical notes, ID documents. Build the roster once, export to PDF for the federation, keep it tidy season after season.
players-roster.heroBullet1: Full player profiles with medical and document fields
players-roster.heroBullet2: GDPR-aware — privacy levels per player and per audience
players-roster.heroBullet3: Federation-ready exports as PDF or CSV
players-roster.painH2: Player data lives in 5 spreadsheets and a folder of PDFs
players-roster.painBody: Each tournament asks for a roster, often in a slightly different format. Coaches re-build the same data over and over, sometimes losing the medical note, sometimes the ID copy. Goality keeps the canonical player roster — your club's gold source — and exports whatever the tournament needs.
players-roster.f1Desc: Name, photo, birth date, nationality, jersey number, position. Validated against division age rules at registration.
players-roster.f2Desc: Allergies, chronic conditions, emergency contact, medication notes. Visible to coaches and medical staff, not public.
players-roster.f3Desc: Passport / ID copies, parental consent, insurance certificate. Uploaded once, attached to the player for all tournaments.
players-roster.f4Desc: Per-player privacy: public name only, public name + jersey, fully hidden. Default protects minors automatically.
players-roster.f5Desc: The player profile carries across tournaments. Update once, every tournament's roster reflects it.
players-roster.f6Desc: Generate the federation-required PDF roster in one click. Sign electronically or print, hand in at registration.
players-roster.q1: How does GDPR work for minors?
players-roster.a1: Players under 16 default to private. Parental consent is captured when the team is created, and personal data is scoped to authorized roles (coach, organizer, medical).
players-roster.q2: Can I bulk-import a roster from Excel?
players-roster.a2: Yes. Upload a CSV with name, birth date, position — Goality maps the fields and shows you a preview before importing.
players-roster.a3: Yes. Stored encrypted at rest, transmitted over HTTPS, visible only to authorized roles. Never appear in public pages or analytics.
players-roster.q4: Can I delete a player's data?
players-roster.a4: Yes. GDPR delete request removes the player from active rosters; data is wiped within 30 days, anonymized statistics retained.
players-roster.ctaBody: Build your club's gold source. Export to whatever the next tournament asks for.

team-shop.metaTitle: Team Shop — Self-Serve Package & Extras Purchase
team-shop.metaDesc: Teams pick their package, add extras and pay online — without 20 emails to the organizer. Stripe-powered, mobile-first.
team-shop.heroSub: A self-serve shop inside the team workspace: pick the package, add extras like extra meals or single rooms, pay online. Organizer sees the deal in real time.
team-shop.heroBullet1: Coaches see only what the organizer published
team-shop.heroBullet2: Extras and adjustments visible per line
team-shop.heroBullet3: Stripe checkout for the team's payment
team-shop.painH2: 20 emails to ask 'can we add a vegetarian meal?'
team-shop.painBody: Without a real shop, every extra request is an email, every payment is a bank transfer photo, every confirmation is a reply that doesn't arrive. Goality gives each team a shop with prices the organizer set, extras the team can add, and a Stripe checkout that closes the loop.
team-shop.f1Desc: The organizer publishes packages — hotel, meals, transfer bundles. Each team sees only published items, draft prices stay hidden.
team-shop.f2Desc: Single room, vegetarian option, late departure, extra training — teams add what they need without contacting the organizer.
team-shop.f3Desc: Every line shows the price, every adjustment is labelled (early-bird discount, sponsor override). No hidden surprises.
team-shop.f4Desc: Subtotal, paid, outstanding — visible on top. Add a line, balance updates. Pay a part, balance updates.
team-shop.f5Desc: Card payment via Stripe. Receipt by email, organizer notification, deal status updates automatically.
team-shop.f6Desc: Pay the deposit now, the balance later. Split between coach and parent committee. Every payment logged in the deal.
team-shop.a1: Card payments via Stripe (Visa, Mastercard, Apple Pay, Google Pay). Bank transfer is supported as a manual option the organizer can enable.
team-shop.a2: Yes. Pay any amount up to the outstanding balance. The deal stays open with the new balance until fully paid.
team-shop.q3: What if the team needs a refund?
team-shop.a3: Refunds run through Stripe. The organizer triggers the refund; the deal updates automatically.
team-shop.q4: Can a coach delegate payment to a parent?
team-shop.a4: Yes. Share the team workspace with a parent who can make the Stripe payment. The deal stays attached to the team, not the payer.
team-shop.ctaBody: Publish your packages. Teams pick, pay, and you see the money.

follow-news.metaTitle: Follow & News — Push Tournament Updates to Parents and Fans
follow-news.metaDesc: Fans, parents and partners follow a tournament with one tap. Get notifications on schedule changes, goals, finals — no app install.
follow-news.heroH1Highlight: pushed to the people who care
follow-news.heroSub: Fans, parents and partners follow with one tap. They get notifications when schedules change, when their team scores, when the final is set — no app to install.
follow-news.heroBullet1: One-tap follow for every tournament, every team
follow-news.heroBullet2: Auto-notifications on schedule changes and results
follow-news.heroBullet3: Manual announcements for finals, ceremonies, weather
follow-news.heroCtaSecondary: Set up news for my tournament
follow-news.painH2: WhatsApp groups don't scale to 500 parents
follow-news.painBody: Once you have 30 teams and 500 parents, the WhatsApp updates stop reaching everyone. Some are kicked out, some don't see notifications, some get duplicate messages. Goality replaces it with a follow system — anyone follows the tournament or a specific team, and gets clean updates without joining a group chat.
follow-news.f1Desc: From any public tournament page, tap follow. Email + push notification setup is done in 5 seconds.
follow-news.f2Desc: Fans can follow just one team — they get notifications only for that team's matches and results.
follow-news.f3Desc: Schedule changes, goals, finals, results — automatic notifications to followers, no manual sending needed.
follow-news.f4Desc: Write announcements with cover image, body and call-to-action. Schedule for later or publish now.
follow-news.f5Desc: See how many followers opened a news post. Improve your messaging based on real reach data.
follow-news.f6Desc: Every email has a one-click unsubscribe. Followers stay opt-in; bounce rates stay low.
follow-news.q1: Do followers need a Goality account?
follow-news.a1: No. Anyone can follow with just an email. Logged-in users get richer notifications and can manage subscriptions in one place.
follow-news.q2: Can I schedule news in advance?
follow-news.a2: Yes. Write an announcement, set a publish time — it goes out automatically. Useful for finals, ceremonies and post-tournament summaries.
follow-news.q3: Do followers get notified about every match?
follow-news.a3: Followers of a team get notifications for their team's events. General tournament followers get summary-level updates (round done, finals announced).
follow-news.q4: Can I broadcast in multiple languages?
follow-news.a4: Yes. Write the post in EN / RU / ET / ES and each follower receives the version in their language.
follow-news.ctaH2: Build the fan base your tournament deserves
follow-news.ctaBody: One-tap follow, clean updates, no group-chat noise.

catalog.metaTitle: Football Tournament Catalog — Find Your Next Cup
catalog.metaDesc: Browse the public Goality catalog: youth, amateur and international football tournaments by country, city and date. Free discovery for teams.
catalog.heroSub: Every public Goality tournament shows up in the catalog — searchable by country, city, age group and date. Free discovery for teams, free reach for organizers.
catalog.heroBullet1: Country and city filters with flags for every tournament
catalog.heroBullet2: Cover image, dates, divisions and registration deadline at a glance
catalog.heroBullet3: Indexed by Google — your tournament gets found organically
catalog.painH2: Teams want to play. They just can't find the right cup
catalog.painBody: International clubs spend hours googling for tournaments in foreign cities. Tournaments spend money on ads to be discovered. The Goality catalog closes that gap — every public tournament shows up automatically, with flags, dates and registration links. Teams find you; you don't pay for discovery.
catalog.f1Desc: Filter by country, city, region. Visiting teams find tournaments near their destination; locals find what's on this weekend.
catalog.f2Desc: Show only U10, U12, U14, women's, or open cups. Coaches stop scrolling past irrelevant tournaments.
catalog.f3Desc: Browse tournaments by month or weekend. Plan a tour, find a free weekend, fill a gap.
catalog.f4Desc: Each tournament card shows cover image, host city flag and the division mix. Visual scanning, not endless text.
catalog.f5Desc: Open / closing soon / full — visible at a glance. Coaches stop wasting time on cups that are already booked.
catalog.f6Desc: Each tournament is server-rendered with structured data. Google finds them; teams searching '[city] football tournament' land on you.
catalog.a1: Yes. Every public tournament is in the catalog at no extra cost. You only pay for the platform tier (Free / Starter / Pro / Elite) — discovery is included.
catalog.q2: Can I hide my tournament from the catalog?
catalog.a2: Yes. Switch it to 'unlisted' — the tournament page stays public via direct link, but it doesn't appear in catalog search.
catalog.a3: Default sort is by date — upcoming first. Tournaments with complete info (cover, regulation, schedule) are prioritized in mixed results.
catalog.q4: Can teams contact the organizer directly from the catalog?
catalog.a4: Yes. Each tournament card links to its public page, which has registration, regulations and a contact channel to the organizer.
catalog.ctaH2: Be found by the teams that want to play
catalog.ctaBody: List your tournament in 5 minutes. Free, indexed by Google, opened to international teams.

security-infrastructure.metaTitle: Security, GDPR & Infrastructure — How Goality Keeps Tournaments Safe
security-infrastructure.metaDesc: GDPR-compliant data handling, encrypted backups, daily R2 + pgBackRest PITR, Hetzner snapshots. Serious tournaments lean on serious infra.
security-infrastructure.heroSub: GDPR-compliant data handling, encrypted backups, point-in-time database restore, daily off-site copies. Built for tournaments that take their data seriously.
security-infrastructure.heroBullet1: GDPR-compliant by design — privacy controls down to per-player
security-infrastructure.heroBullet2: Daily backups to Cloudflare R2 + pgBackRest WAL streaming
security-infrastructure.painH2: A federation can't trust software that runs on someone's laptop
security-infrastructure.painBody: Federations, schools and serious leagues need to know where the data lives, who can access it, and how fast you recover from disaster. Goality runs on dedicated infrastructure with multi-layer backups, GDPR-grade privacy controls and a transparent operations log — so the answer to 'is it safe?' is documented, not vibes.
security-infrastructure.featuresH2: How we keep your tournament safe
security-infrastructure.f1Desc: Per-player privacy levels, parental consent workflow, right-to-erasure within 30 days, anonymized analytics. Audit-ready.
security-infrastructure.f2Desc: Database storage encrypted at rest, HTTPS-only transport, sensitive fields (medical, ID) extra-protected. No casual eavesdropping.
security-infrastructure.f3Desc: Nightly backups streamed to Cloudflare R2 in a separate region. If the primary server burns down, the data isn't lost.
security-infrastructure.f4Desc: pgBackRest WAL streaming means we can restore the database to any second in the recent past. Wrong delete? Recovered in minutes.
security-infrastructure.f5Desc: Hetzner server snapshots layered on top of database backups. Triple redundancy: WAL + R2 + Hetzner.
security-infrastructure.f6Desc: Every deploy runs CI, builds a Docker image, atomically swaps on the server. Monitored via @goalityTMC_bot. Rollback is one command.
security-infrastructure.a1: Primary database lives on a Hetzner server in the EU. Daily backups stream to Cloudflare R2 (also EU region). No transatlantic data shipping.
security-infrastructure.a2: WAL streaming gives us a recovery point of seconds. In a worst-case full restore, expect minutes — not hours of lost work.
security-infrastructure.q3: Can I export all my tournament data?
security-infrastructure.a3: Yes. Roster, schedule, results, payments — exportable as CSV or JSON. You always own your data.
security-infrastructure.a4: Players under 16 require parental consent (captured electronically). Personal data is scoped to authorized roles, never appears in public pages, and is deletable within 30 days on request.
security-infrastructure.ctaH2: Run your tournament on serious infrastructure
security-infrastructure.ctaBody: Encrypted, backed up, GDPR-ready — without lifting a finger. Free to start.
